course_title: World Literature I: Myths and Traditions
description:
Offered: May be less than once every two years
This course presents a selection of works from around the world, from ancient traditions up to the 16th century. We explore aspects of the human experience and how they are reflected in the literary traditions of these time periods. Focus is on the JudeoChristian and Greco-Roman foundations of Western literature, the Confucian, Hindu/Sanskrit, Buddhist, and Islamic underpinnings of literature in Africa, Asia, and the Middle East, and the colonization of North America.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Note: Students who have declared an English Studies major may take concurrently with ENGL 1200 with department approval.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, DP, LI
